title: eriocaulon quinquangulare subsp. longibracteatum
intro: Eriocaulon quinquangulare subsp. longibracteatum, a type of plant, is a sub-species of Eriocaulon quinquangulare, a genus of flowering plants in the family Eriocaulaceae. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Eriocaulon quinquangulare subsp. longibracteatum is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
